_meta.derivation: earliest commit per author_login from commits.json; enrich via /commits/{sha} for files/stats

data.author: selimrecep
data.message:
fix: guard IPv6 functions with DDSRT_HAVE_IPV6 preprocessor checks

When building with ENABLE_IPV6=OFF, ownaddrs_hash6 and ownaddrs_eq6
reference union addr members (a6) that don't exist since the IPv6
sockaddr_in6 field is conditionally compiled out. This causes compile
errors on the hash/eq functions and the else branch in ddsi_udp_init.

Wrap ownaddrs_hash6, ownaddrs_eq6 and their usage in ddsi_udp_init
with #if DDSRT_HAVE_IPV6 guards. When IPv6 is disabled, only the
IPv4 hash table path is compiled.

Fixes #2351

Signed-off-by: Recep Selim Ağırman <recepselimagirman@gmail.com>

data.author: dpotman
data.message:
Fix compilation errors for RMW build on Windows (#248)

* Fix compilation errors for RMW build on Windows

This commit fixes the compilation errors that occur when building
the ROS2 rmw_cyclonedds_cpp module on Windows with the msvc C++
compiler. The error are fixed by adding explicit casts when calling
operations on atomics and a different syntax is used for compound
literals (the c99 syntax is not supported in msvc in C++ mode).

In additionally some warnings related to emtpy array usage
in structs are suppressed for msvc.

Signed-off-by: Dennis Potman <dennis.potman@adlinktech.com>

* Restructured macros for vendor id cast and casts for atomic operations

To improve readability I've restructured the macros that are used
for vendor id casts on msvc in c++ mode and macros for type-casting
arguments in operations on atomics (as suggested in the review of
my previous commit)

Signed-off-by: Dennis Potman <dennis.potman@adlinktech.com>

* Moved atomic function suffix to macro

Moved the suffix for (msvc) 64 bit atomic functions to the
DDSRT_ATOMIC_OP64 macro

Signed-off-by: Dennis Potman <dennis.potman@adlinktech.com>

data.author: reicheratwork
data.message:
Replacing the separate iox-c binding with the integrated implementation

- the concept of sending a block of data preceded by a header is continued
- only "flat" data structures are supported at this instance, to not have
to use the cdr (de-)serialization in sending and receiving iox blocks
- added a monitoring thread which waits on the subscribers signalling
they have data available through a waitset
- this then calls the appropriate reader functions for the received data
- added the dummy export header features.h, which has the DDS_HAS_SHM define
set when the cmake flag ENABLE_SHM is set to On
- added the serdata/sertopic iox utility functions
  - ddsi_serdata_iox_size_t: returns the "flat" size of the sample to be sent
  - ddsi_serdata_from_iox_t: constructs a serdata from a received iox block

Signed-off-by: Martijn Reicher <martijn.reicher@adlinktech.com>

data.author: PeterCadogan
data.message:
Documentation edits to the Getting Started Section (#1510)

* Documentation edits to the Getting Started Section

I've made changes to the Installation and Benchmarking sections. I've also split these sections into separate files.

* Updated with review comments

* Edits to the Configuration Guide

* Edits to the DDSI Concepts page

As this was a large topic, with multiple sections, I split it into several files.

* Update docs/manual/config/ddsi-transient_behavoir.rst

Co-authored-by: Phil <25635927+poetinger@users.noreply.github.com>

* Edits to the Network configuration page

I've separated the Network configuration section into its own page.

In addition, I've updated the log on the Getting started page so that it is smaller, and has a white background.

I've also started to add index and glossary entries. The definitions for  the glossary items need to be defined - see https://github.com/orgs/ZettaScaleLabs/projects/5/views/2?visibleFields=%5B%22Title%22%2C23825276%2C%22Assignees%22%2C%22Status%22%5D

* Edits to the Data path configuration

In addition to the edits, I have put all the external html links into a separate file. Hopefully, this will make it easier to check for broken or superseded links.

* Edits to the Network partition configuration

This includes updates to cross-references, index and glossary

* Edits to the Examples pages

* Edits to the Security pages

I've split the security pages into separate files so that they appear in the left-hand TOC

* Further edits to the Configuration pages

I've separated the Network configuration into separate topics.

* Edits to the Shared memory section

I've split the Shared memory page into separate files and moved it to a Shared memory folder. I've also updated the Getting started logo to a scalable svg.

* Updated in response to review comments

Mostly, minor changes to capitalisation. Also some fixes to the Network configuration pages

* Added a introduction section and merged HelloWorld tutorials into a single tutorial

I've added a top-level section that has much the same information as the intro in the GitHub website (README.md). This also includes other high-level topics such as the data-centric architecture.

Because the HelloWorld C core DDS and C++ HelloWorld tutorials follow the same pattern, with similar explanations (except for the code examples), I've merged them into a single tutorial, with tab groups for the C and C++ specific areas.

* Updates to the Core DDS (C) tutorial

The code in the Core DDS (C) tutorial did not match the subscriber.c and the publisher.c code samples in the Examples > HelloWorld. Therefore, I've replaced the code with a literalinclude so that these remain in sync. For the explanatory parts of the tutorial, I've replaced the snippets with the code from subscriber.c and the publisher.c, including line numbers so that it's easier to reference them.

* Further updates to the C++ tutorial

I've update the C++ tutorials so that it matches the publisher.cpp and subscriber.cpp files

* Added CMake build configuration

This was in the README.md file, so I've added it to the Main documentation. Includes links to other relevant pages.

* Updated external links to OMG IDL

* Corrected a typo in the external links page

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: NoxPardalis <NoxPardalis@users.noreply.github.com>

* Updates to the threashold example

* Updated "Cyclone DDS" to use variable references

* Update to platforms.rst

* Update to prerequisites.rst

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: NoxPardalis <NoxPardalis@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Phil <25635927+poetinger@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: NoxPardalis <NoxPardalis@users.noreply.github.com>

data.author: James Pace
data.message:
Add fix suggested in sros2 bug report.

As stated in: https://github.com/OpenDDS/OpenDDS/pull/3992#issue-1548228907

"Implement the suggestion in ros2/sros2#282.
Specifically, use PKCS7_NOINTERN to not accept any signatures in the signed document.
This, in turn, requires the use of the certs parameter to PKCS7_verify.
PKCS7_NOVERIFY is used since the permissions CA certificate will not be chain verified."

Fixes: https://github.com/eclipse-cyclonedds/cyclonedds/issues/1546
Related to: https://github.com/ros2/sros2/issues/282

Signed-off-by: James Pace <jpace121@gmail.com>

data.author: cottsay
data.message:
Add a colcon.pkg file to override the CMake package name (#242)

* Add a colcon.pkg file to override the CMake package name

Without this file, colcon will take the project name from the
CMakeLists.txt file, which is `CycloneDDS`. This name does not conform
to the standard practices for a ROS package name, and should be
lowercase (i.e. `cyclonedds`).

This `colcon.pkg` will make colcon detect the package dependencies using
this name.

Signed-off-by: Scott K Logan <logans@cottsay.net>

* Drop unneeded package type

This isn't needed as colcon can detect the package type as `cmake`
without it.

Signed-off-by: Scott K Logan <logans@cottsay.net>

data.author: lopsided98
data.message:
Fix assumptions that CMAKE_INSTALL_*DIR variables are relative

The CMAKE_INSTALL_*DIR variables are allowed to be absolute paths, which allows
libraries and headers to be installed into separate prefixes. This means they
cannot be naively concatenated with CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX. If an absolute path
is needed, CMAKE_INSTALL_FULL_*DIR should be used, while more complicated
handling is required to keep ${prefix} interpolation in pkg-config files in
the case where CMAKE_INSTALL_*DIR is relative.

Signed-off-by: Ben Wolsieffer <benwolsieffer@gmail.com>

data.author: ahoenerBE
data.message:
(idlc) Add options for output directory and path reconstruction / prefix (#1263)

* Add options for output directory and path reconstruction (#1220)

Added -o command line option to specify an output directory which may or may not exist.
Added -b command line option to specify a base path for a directory structure.
When using -b, any directories from <base_dir> to <file> will be reconstructed in the output directory. If no output directory is specified, this will result in files being compiled in-place.
Necessary directories will be constructed automatically to hold the output files

Signed-off-by: Andrew Hoener <ahoener@boston-engineering.com>

* Extracted path generation to its own functions.
Added unit tests

Signed-off-by: Andrew Hoener <ahoener@boston-engineering.com>

* Updated Generate.cmake to use the new output_dir by default.
Added BASE_DIR option to specify the base dir for the program

Signed-off-by: Andrew Hoener <ahoener@boston-engineering.com>

data.author: Dennis Potman
data.message:
Liveliness QoS implementation for auto and manual-by-participant

This commit adds support for the liveliness QoS for the liveliness
kinds automatic and manual-by-participant. It also implements the
lease_duration from this QoS, which was ignored until now. In the
api the function dds_assert_liveliness is added to assert liveliness
on a participant, which can be used when using liveliness kind
manual-by-participant.

Liveliness kind manual-by-topic is not yet supported, this will be
added in a later commit.

* Proxy participants now have 2 fibheaps to keep leases: one for leases
of pwrs with automatic liveliness and one for leases of the pwrs with
manual-by-participant liveliness (both protected by the proxypp lock).
The minl_auto and minl_man members represent the shortest lease from
these fibheaps and these leases are renewed when receiving data.
Replacing the minl_ leases is now done by replacing the lease object
(atomic ptr) with delayed deletion of the old lease using the gc.

* Proxy writers are set not-alive when the lease expired, and reset to
alive then data is received. When data is received by a pwr, the other
pwrs in the proxypp might also be set alive. I think the specification
is not clear at this point, and for now I have not implemented this

* I refactored out the counter for man-by-pp proxy writers and improved
locking when updating the min-leases on the proxy participant, so I
think this fixes the race conditions.

Some additional tests are required, e.g. to test the not-alive->alive
transition for pwrs. I will add these in short term, as well as the
implementation of the manual-by-topic liveliness kind.

Signed-off-by: Dennis Potman <dennis.potman@adlinktech.com>

data.author: stkimmer
data.message:
Certificate trigger and directory operations

Implement trigger of certificate and permission expiries using the timed callbacks.

Implement directory operations such that trusted CA can be read.
This implements OS abstraction functions such as opendir and stat.

Signed-off-by: Stefan Kimmer <skimmer@s2e-systems.com>

data.author: jrebelo
data.message:
Add timed callback dispatcher to security core

Dispatchers are added to a custom linked list while each dispatcher owns a
fibheap of callbacks that are ordered by expiry timestamp. A seperate
fibheap is use for each dispatcher to allow disabling and enabling of
each dispatcher. A new and free function is added that creates a
timed_cd_data object that is used by every public function. A thread is
initialized at object creation instead of using a counter. Add
a protection for the terminate flag.

Signed-off-by: Joao Rebelo <jrebelo@s2e-systems.com>

data.author: kurtuluso
data.message:
Security API has been added for OMG DDS Security Specification compliance.
This API consists of only header files which are grabbed from spec IDL. The dynamically loaded plugins should implement API functions.

Built-in plugins will be added to the repository later. Third party plugins will also be able to be integrated.
